msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 140 is another attachment-only continuation. The required current-turn attachments are present: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md. I did not use or substitute any File Library material.

Turn 140 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The target remains exactly the over-G question for the two profinite one-relator quotients ΓS,ΓH: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.This is the condition stated in the attached problem statement.

The two-coordinate state remains unchanged from Turn 139:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed inside the attached sweep. The history records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement:∃Φ:F∼

F over GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.

This is exactly the requested condition πH∘Φ=πS.No structural computation should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remainsH1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history identifies the uniform reason: non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The compactness coordinate remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one. For coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms. Certificates imply SN,J=∅; functoriality and compactness give a compatible inverse-limit datum, hence a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt/problem/history set; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1--D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED.

Thus Turn 140 is idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 140 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 139. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1--D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1--D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN140-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
